etymology.description: semantic 广(廣) (guǎng, "broad") + phonetic 黃(黃) (huáng, "yellow") -> broad
etymology.interpretation: The character 廣 (guǎng, "broad") combines the semantic element 广(廣), depicting a broad area or structure, with the phonetic element 黃(黃) (huáng), which lends its sound. This combination signifies the idea of something wide or expansive.
etymology.simplification: 廣 was simplified to 广 by removing the 黃(黃) (huáng, "yellow") phonetic component, leaving only the semantic element.

examples.chinese: 这条河非常广阔(這條河非常廣闊)。
他的知识面很广(他的知識面很廣)，总是能解决问题(總是能解決問題)。
examples.pinyin: Tā de zhīshì miàn hěn guǎng, zǒng shì néng jiějué wèntí.
examples.english: His knowledge is broad, and he is always able to solve problems.

examples.chinese: 在学校里(在學校裡)，他参加了许多广泛的活动(他參加了許多廣泛的活動)。
examples.pinyin: Zài xuéxiào lǐ, tā cānjiāle xǔduō guǎngfàn de huódòng.
examples.english: At school, he participated in many broad activities.
